acceptance_targets: [CH10_CONTINUITY_TRAY_STATE] Pass only if a cold reader can track Viktor's tray/bowl cleanly from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:71 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:133 without any contradiction. Fail if the revised text still has him eat from the bowl in the cafeteria and later treats the same serving as untouched upstairs.

[CH10_STRUCTURAL_RECURRENCE_VIKTOR] Pass only if the revised lab passage at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:157 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:209 either produces one new public consequence beyond "Viktor is ignored again" or trims the repeated appeal so the scene reaches Tomas's message without multiple paragraphs re-litigating food, rooms, and intervention. Fail if the reader still leaves snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:209 with no material state change beyond a second certification of Lena's existing control.
[TENSE_DRIFT_CH10] Pass if the description uses present perfect or present tense. Fail if simple past 'went gray' remains.

[SOMATIC_VOCAB_CH10] Pass if the 40-line span from 2793-2832 uses 'mouth' no more than once and pairs it with observations from other physical systems. Fail if three mouth/jaw descriptions cluster in the same span.
[TRANSITION_PAUSE_CH10] Pass if 'shift' appears no more than once and 'for a second/beat' appears no more than once. Fail if either construction exceeds one occurrence.
[RED-017] Ch10 does not describe objects as 'colder than yesterday' or restate the progressive cooling trend.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can track Viktor's tray/bowl cleanly from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:71 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:133 without any contradiction. Fail if the revised text still has him eat from the bowl in the cafeteria and later treats the same serving as untouched upstairs.
findings.problem: Viktor's breakfast tray breaks continuity at a conspicuous moment. The chapter shows him taking a tray and eating halfway through the bowl downstairs, then later describes him setting an "untouched tray" against the corridor wall upstairs. That kind of object-state contradiction is easy for a reader to catch and undercuts trust in the chapter's physical bookkeeping.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile the tray from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:71, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:73, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:133. Either keep Viktor carrying a visibly half-eaten bowl into the third-floor corridor, or leave the tray in the cafeteria and replace the upstairs beat with a different physical tell for his shaking hands. Do not let an already eaten bowl become "untouched."

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised lab passage at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:157 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:209 either produces one new public consequence beyond "Viktor is ignored again" or trims the repeated appeal so the scene reaches Tomas's message without multiple paragraphs re-litigating food, rooms, and intervention. Fail if the reader still leaves snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:209 with no material state change beyond a second certification of Lena's existing control.
findings.problem: By the time the chapter returns to the lab, Lena's rule and Viktor's impotence have already been proved in public through the cafeteria confrontation and Jun's killing. The lab beat then spends multiple paragraphs having Viktor restate food, room, and intervention principles in cleaner governance language before the Tomás interruption arrives. That turns the second scene into a re-argument of a point the chapter had already landed instead of buying a genuinely new turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the lab follow-up centered at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:157, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:163, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:169, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:191, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:199, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:209. Either compress Viktor's repeated governance appeal to a single failed bid, or give the sequence a new irreversible public consequence before Tomas's chime cuts across it. The scene needs fresh dramatic work, not a cleaner thematic translation of what snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:49 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:141 already established.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the description uses present perfect or present tense. Fail if simple past 'went gray' remains.

evidence.excerpt: 2593: He is still in the blue button-down from Day 1, washed in a third-floor sink until the collar went gray. One cuff is frayed open and dark with old water. His hands are already moving when he says Lena's name. Behind him, one of the six is looking at the serving line instead of at Lena.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2593: He is still in the blue button-down from Day 1, washed in a third-floor sink until the collar went gray. One cuff is frayed open and dark with old water. His hands are already moving when he says Lena's name. Behind him, one of the six is looking at the serving line instead of at Lena.

findings.problem: Past-tense slip in present-tense description: 'washed in a third-floor sink until the collar went gray' describes a visible current condition using simple past.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2593, convert to present perfect: 'washed in a third-floor sink until the collar has gone gray.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the 40-line span from 2793-2832 uses 'mouth' no more than once and pairs it with observations from other physical systems. Fail if three mouth/jaw descriptions cluster in the same span.

evidence.excerpt: 2793: Noor notices because stillness is wrong on him. Usually some part of him is in motion, tapping, turning, smoothing nothing. Now both hands are flat on the desk. His jaw shifts once, as if he's chewing the inside of his cheek. He reads without blinking. Then he reads the same lines again, slower.

2804: Noor keeps walking. At the cooler she fills the bottle, caps it, and looks back through the glass. Tomás is still reading. His hand covers his mouth. When it drops, his mouth stays slack for a beat. His eyes move over the same four lines again, as if reading them twice might put him back in control.

2832: Something comes out in Russian, maybe words, maybe just breath trying to shape itself before it runs out. Noor shifts her hands and feels the wound open slickly under the cloth, not a clean puncture but something torn wider on the way in or out. Blood wells around her thumbs in dark pulses that get farther apart and then close together again when he coughs. A pink froth gathers at the corner of his mouth. He tries to swallow and can't.

findings.problem: 'Mouth' appears in three rapid-fire physical descriptions: 'His jaw shifts once' (2793), 'his mouth stays slack for a beat' (2804), 'A pink froth gathers at the corner of his mouth. He tries to swallow and can't' (2832). The somatic vocabulary narrows to mouth/jaw/swallow in the chapter's most intense passages.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2793-2832, keep the death detail at :2832 (the froth is specific and effective). At :2804, replace 'his mouth stays slack' with a different physical observation — posture change, hand position, breath pattern. At :2793, 'His jaw shifts' is acceptable if 'mouth' at 2804 is replaced.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if 'shift' appears no more than once and 'for a second/beat' appears no more than once. Fail if either construction exceeds one occurrence.

evidence.excerpt: 2643: The woman with the cane goes next. Then Soyeon. Then one of Viktor's own people, a tall man with a split lower lip who does not even try to make the choice look temporary. Nobody announces the shift. They step into line because the food is here and Viktor can't stop anybody.

2787: He goes to the whiteboard by the printer, uncaps a dry marker, and writes ROOMS / FOOD / INTERVENTION in letters that fade before he reaches the last stroke. He stares at the gray imprint for a second, recaps the marker, and leaves the room.

2793: Noor notices because stillness is wrong on him. Usually some part of him is in motion, tapping, turning, smoothing nothing. Now both hands are flat on the desk. His jaw shifts once, as if he's chewing the inside of his cheek. He reads without blinking. Then he reads the same lines again, slower.

2804: Noor keeps walking. At the cooler she fills the bottle, caps it, and looks back through the glass. Tomás is still reading. His hand covers his mouth. When it drops, his mouth stays slack for a beat. His eyes move over the same four lines again, as if reading them twice might put him back in control.
2916: He lets go and sits back on his heels for a second, breathing through his nose. Then he stands and wipes both hands on his shirt.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2643: The woman with the cane goes next. Then Soyeon. Then one of Viktor's own people, a tall man with a split lower lip who does not even try to make the choice look temporary. Nobody announces the shift. They step into line because the food is here and Viktor can't stop anybody.

findings.problem: 'Shift' appears three times (2643, 2793, 2832) and 'for a second/beat' appears three times (2787, 2804, 2916). Six instances of these default constructions in one chapter create a highly repetitive transition-and-pause cadence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2643, replace 'Nobody announces the shift' with 'Nobody announces the change' or 'Nobody announces it.' At :2787 and :2916, delete 'for a second' — let the pause live in the sentence break or a physical detail. At :2804, replace 'for a beat' with action: 'His eyes move over the same four lines again' already provides the pause. Keep :2793 as the single 'shift' instance if needed.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch10 does not describe objects as 'colder than yesterday' or restate the progressive cooling trend.
findings.evidence: Ch9 L2356: 'The surface is cold, colder than yesterday. The building has been cooling since sometime on Day 7'; Ch10 L2940: 'The keys are colder than yesterday. The building keeps cooling around the bodies.'
findings.problem: Temperature/cold baseline re-proved at Ch10 L2940 without materially new consequence. Part of a four-chapter cluster (Ch10-13) that re-proves 'the building is getting colder' through incidental physical details (cold keys, cold floors, cold fabric) without new plot consequence, data, or mechanism. The cold baseline was analytically established in Ch9 via Suki's temperature analysis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or compress the cold reference at L2940. Reserve cold language for the Ch14 escalation (Priya's graph) and Ch15 frost/ice imagery.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
